source: bpb summary of BKA PKS 2024 Table 61
method_note: Direct published migrant-suspect count for Körperverletzung (assault/body injury). Used as the closest nationwide migrant-only violence figure publicly surfaced in the cited BKA/BPB material.

source: bpb summary of BKA PKS 2024 Table 61
method_note: Direct published migrant-suspect count for Diebstahl (theft). Used as the closest nationwide migrant-only property-crime count publicly available in the cited source.

source: BKA T62 burglary non-German suspects + BKA/BMI totals for migrants among non-German suspects
method_note: Estimate. Search snippet for BKA T62 gives 10,180 non-German burglary suspects for Wohnungseinbruchdiebstahl. Multiplied by the 2024 nationwide migrant share within all non-German suspects: 172,203 / 913,196 = 18.86%.

source: Mediendienst Integration summary of BKA Bundeslagebild 2024
method_note: Proxy rate. The cited source reports that migrants accounted for 12.3% of suspects in Vermögens- und Fälschungsdelikte in 2024. Used as the closest published nationwide migrant rate for the fraud/property-forgery bucket.

source: Destatis prosecution statistics + BKA migrant suspect total
method_note: Estimate. Destatis reports that about 60% of all criminal investigations were dismissed in 2024. Germany does not publish a migrant-specific dismissal series in the cited source, so the national dismissal rate was applied to the 172,203 migrant suspects as a proxy.

source: World Prison Brief / official prison administration compilation
method_note: Proxy. This is the share of foreign prisoners in Germany on 31 Jan 2024. Germany does not publish a standard prison-population percentage by migrant background in the cited official-style series, so foreign-prisoner share is used as the closest available proxy.

source: BKA Bundeslagebild 2024 age distribution of migrant suspects
method_note: Estimate based on BKA's reported 2024 age distribution of migrant suspects: 10.6% were Jugendliche. Applied to the total migrant suspect count of 172,203.

source: BKA domestic-violence appendix snippet + BKA/BMI migrant suspect share
method_note: Estimate. Germany does not publish a clean nationwide migrant-only count for §235 minor-abduction cases in the sources I could verify. I used the 2024 BKA appendix snippet showing 2,734 'Entziehung Minderjähriger' cases, then applied the official 2024 migrant share of all suspects (8.8%) to force a discrete number without fabricating a fake direct statistic.

source: BMI/BKA PKS 2024 child sexual abuse total + BKA/BMI migrant suspect share
method_note: Estimate. PKS 2024 reports 16,354 cases of 'sexueller Missbrauch von Kindern'. The same 2024 PKS/BKA release states migrants were 8.8% of all suspects (excluding immigration-law offenses). Because I could not verify a direct nationwide migrant-only child-sex-offense count from official open sources, I applied the official migrant suspect share to the national child-sex-abuse case count.
